author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
originalBook: Treatise on the Soul and Its Origin
title: Victor’s Third Quotation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: He proceeds to favour us with a third passage, in which it is written: “Who forms the spirit of man within him.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: As if any one denied this!

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: No; all our question is as to the mode of the formation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Now let us take the eye of the body, and ask, who but God forms it?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: I suppose that He forms it not externally, but in itself, and yet, most certainly, by propagation.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Soul and its Origin.
verses.text: Since, then, He also forms “the human spirit in him,” the question still remains, whether it be derived by a fresh insufflation in every instance, or by propagation.
